title: Performance: auto-insert memory prefetch hints for FF-driven array reads
body: ## Summary When simulating designs with large arrays whose read address is driven by a flip-flop (registered address), Verilator could automatically insert `VL_PREFETCH_RD` at the end of the generated `_nba_sequent` function. This gives one full simulation cycle of prefetch lead time and significantly reduces DRAM latency stalls. ## Example ```verilog // A simplified pipelined hash lookup module hash_lookup ( input wire clk, input wire [31:0] query, output reg [31:0] result ); reg [31:0] mem [0:4194303]; reg [31:0] addr_q; always @(posedge clk) begin addr_q <= (query ^ (query >> 16)) & 22'h3FFFFF; result <= mem[addr_q]; end endmodule ``` After eval(), addr_q has been committed to hash(query_this_cycle). That value is the index mem[] will use next cycle. A prefetch inserted here gets one full simulation cycle of lead time. --- Current generated code (no prefetch) In _nba_sequent: ``` vlSelf->addr_q = (vlSelf->query ^ (vlSelf->query >> 16)) & 0xFFFFFFU; vlSelf->result = vlSelf->mem[vlSelf->addr_q]; // <-- nothing here; next cycle's mem[] access will cold-miss ``` Expected / proposed generated code ``` vlSelf->addr_q = (vlSelf->query ^ (vlSelf->query >> 16)) & 0xFFFFFFU; vlSelf->result = vlSelf->mem[vlSelf->addr_q]; // auto-inserted when array exceeds size threshold and addr is a FF: VL_PREFETCH_RD(&vlSelf->mem[vlSelf->addr_q]); ``` --- Benchmark results The benchmark (`bench_hash.py`, can be found in the end of this description) is self-contained: it generates the Verilog, compiles with Verilator, and measures the speedup when prefetch is manually inserted in the testbench (simulating the proposed auto-insertion). Run with: cd test_example && python3 bench_hash.py Results on Apple M2 (L3 = 12MB): --- Detection conditions The optimization applies when, within an always @(posedge clk) block: 1. An array mem[addr] is read (non-blocking assignment RHS) 2. addr is also written in the same block (confirming it is a FF) 3. The array byte size exceeds a threshold This seems detectable via AST analysis before or during code emission. --- Version Verilator 5.043 devel rev v5.042-214-g32dafdcc6 OS macOS 15 (Apple M2) --- May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself? Yes — happy to implement this if the approach is agreed upon. --- bench_hash.py: ```python3 #!/usr/bin/env python3 """ Benchmark: cross-cycle prefetch for FF-driven array addresses. Typical IC pattern: pipelined hash lookup. Stage 1: addr_q <= hash(query) [address pipeline register / FF] Stage 2: result <= mem[addr_q] [memory read with registered address] After eval() for cycle N, addr_q has been committed to hash(query_N). That is exactly the address that mem[] will use next cycle. """ import os import subprocess import sys try: import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import matplotlib.ticker as ticker except ImportError: subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "install", "matplotlib"], check=True) import matplotlib.pyplot as plt import matplotlib.ticker as ticker VERILATOR = "../bin/verilator" WORKDIR = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__)) NUM_CYCLES = 2_000_000 ARRAY_SIZES = [16384, 65536, 262144, 1048576, 4194304, 16777216, 67108864, 134217728] CACHE_L1 = 32 * 1024 CACHE_L2 = 512 * 1024 CACHE_L3 = 12 * 1024 * 1024 ELEM_BYTES = 4 # ── Verilog generator ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────── def make_verilog(tag, depth, mask): return ( "// Pipelined hash-lookup - FF-driven address pattern.\n" "// addr_q is a pipeline register updated each cycle.\n" "// After eval(), addr_q == next cycle's mem[] index.\n" f"module hash_lookup_{tag} #(\n" f" parameter DEPTH = {depth},\n" f" parameter MASK = {mask}\n" ") (\n" " input wire clk,\n" " input wire [31:0] query,\n" " output reg [31:0] result\n" ");\n" " reg [31:0] mem [0:DEPTH-1];\n" " reg [31:0] addr_q;\n" "\n" " always @(posedge clk) begin\n" " addr_q <= (query ^ (query >> 16)) & MASK;\n" " result <= mem[addr_q & MASK];\n" " end\n" "endmodule\n" ) # ── Testbench generator ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────── def make_tb(tag, mask, cycles): cls = f"Vhash_lookup_{tag}" dot = f"hash_lookup_{tag}__DOT__" return ( "// Modes: rnd (no prefetch) | cross (cross-cycle prefetch)\n" "// cross: after eval(), prefetch mem[addr_q].\n" "// Simulates VL_PREFETCH_RD auto-inserted at end of _nba_sequent.\n" "#include \n" f"#include \"{cls}.h\"\n" f"#include \"{cls}___024root.h\"\n" "#include \n" "#include \n" "#include \n" "#include \n" "\n" "static uint32_t lcg(uint32_t x) { return x * 1664525u + 1013904223u; }\n" "\n" "int main(int argc, char** argv) {\n" " const bool cross = (argc > 1 && strcmp(argv[1], \"cross\") == 0);\n" " VerilatedContext* ctx = new VerilatedContext;\n" f" {cls}* top = new {cls}{{ctx}};\n" " top->clk = 0; top->eval();\n" " uint32_t sq = 1;\n" " volatile uint32_t sink = 0;\n" " struct timespec t0, t1;\n" " clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t0);\n" f" for (int c = 0; c < {cycles}; c++) {{\n" " sq = lcg(sq);\n" " top->query = sq;\n" " top->clk = 0; top->eval();\n" " top->clk = 1; top->eval();\n" " sink ^= top->result;\n" " if (cross) {\n" " // Cross-cycle prefetch:\n" " // addr_q now holds hash(query_this_cycle) = next cycle's index.\n" " // Equivalent to: VL_PREFETCH_RD(&vlSelf->mem[vlSelf->addr_q]);\n" " __builtin_prefetch(\n" f" &top->rootp->{dot}mem[\n" f" top->rootp->{dot}addr_q & {mask}u], 0, 0);\n" " }\n" " }\n" " clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t1);\n" " double ms = (t1.tv_sec - t0.tv_sec)*1e3 + (t1.tv_nsec - t0.tv_nsec)*1e-6;\n" " printf(\"%.3f\\n\", ms);\n" " (void)sink;\n" " top->final(); delete top; delete ctx;\n" " return 0;\n" "}\n" ) # ── helpers ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── def run(cmd, **kw): r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, **kw) if r.returncode != 0: print("STDERR:", r.stderr[-2000:]) raise RuntimeError(f"Command failed: {' '.join(cmd)}") return r def bench(depth): mask = depth - 1 tag = str(depth) odir = os.path.join(WORKDIR, f"obj_hash_{tag}") vf = os.path.join(WORKDIR, f"hash_lookup_{tag}.v") tbf = os.path.join(WORKDIR, f"tb_hash_{tag}.cpp") exe = os.path.join(odir, f"sim_hash_{tag}") if not os.path.exists(exe): with open(vf, "w") as f: f.write(make_verilog(tag, depth, mask)) with open(tbf, "w") as f: f.write(make_tb(tag, mask, NUM_CYCLES)) run([VERILATOR, "--cc", "--exe", "--build", "-o", f"sim_hash_{tag}", "--Mdir", odir, vf, tbf], cwd=WORKDIR) ms_rnd = float(run([exe, "rnd"], cwd=WORKDIR).stdout.strip()) ms_cross = float(run([exe, "cross"], cwd=WORKDIR).stdout.strip()) return ms_rnd, ms_cross # ── main ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── def main(): rnd_results = {} cross_results = {} for depth in ARRAY_SIZES: mem_kb = depth * ELEM_BYTES / 1024 label = f"{mem_kb/1024:.0f}MB" if mem_kb >= 1024 else f"{mem_kb:.0f}KB" print(f" depth={depth:>10} ({label:>6}) ... ", end="", flush=True) try: ms_rnd, ms_cross = bench(depth) rnd_results[depth] = ms_rnd cross_results[depth] = ms_cross speedup = ms_rnd / ms_cross print(f"rnd={ms_rnd:7.0f}ms cross={ms_cross:7.0f}ms speedup={speedup:.2f}x") except Exception as e: print(f"FAILED: {e}") if not rnd_results: print("No results to plot.") return # ── plot ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(15, 6)) fig.suptitle( "Verilator: Cross-cycle Prefetch for FF-driven Array Access\n" f"Pattern: pipelined hash lookup ({NUM_CYCLES:,} cycles, 1 lookup/cycle)\n" "addr_q (FF) updated each cycle => address known at end of eval()", fontsize=11, fontweight="bold" ) xs = sorted(rnd_results) ax1.plot(xs, [rnd_results[x] for x in xs], "o-", color="#F44336", linewidth=2, markersize=7, markerfacecolor="white", markeredgewidth=2, label="No prefetch (current Verilator)") ax1.plot(xs, [cross_results[x] for x in xs], "^-", color="#9C27B0", linewidth=2, markersize=7, markerfacecolor="white", markeredgewidth=2, label="Cross-cycle prefetch\n(proposed auto-insert at end of _nba_sequent)") ax1.set_xscale("log") ax1.set_xlabel("Table depth (32-bit entries)", fontsize=10) ax1.set_ylabel("Runtime (ms)", fontsize=10) ax1.set_title("Runtime", fontsize=10) ax1.legend(fontsize=9) ax1.grid(True, alpha=0.3) ax1.xaxis.set_major_formatter(ticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{int(x):,}")) speedups = [rnd_results[x] / cross_results[x] for x in xs] ax2.plot(xs, speedups, "s-", color="#2196F3", linewidth=2, markersize=7, markerfacecolor="white", markeredgewidth=2) ax2.axhline(1.0, color="gray", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5) ax2.set_xscale("log") ax2.set_xlabel("Table depth (32-bit entries)", fontsize=10) ax2.set_ylabel("Speedup (x)", fontsize=10) ax2.set_title("Speedup from cross-cycle prefetch", fontsize=10) ax2.grid(True, alpha=0.3) ax2.xaxis.set_major_formatter(ticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{int(x):,}")) for ax in (ax1, ax2): for cache_bytes, lbl, color in [ (CACHE_L1, "L1", "#4CAF50"), (CACHE_L2, "L2", "#FF9800"), (CACHE_L3, "L3", "#E91E63"), ]: boundary = cache_bytes / ELEM_BYTES if min(xs) <= boundary <= max(xs): ax.axvline(boundary, color=color, linestyle="--", alpha=0.5, linewidth=1.5) ylim = ax.get_ylim() ax.text(boundary * 1.05, ylim[1] * 0.97, lbl, color=color, fontsize=9, va="top", fontweight="bold") plt.tight_layout() out_png = os.path.join(WORKDIR, "bench_hash.png") plt.savefig(out_png, dpi=150) print(f"\nChart saved -> {out_png}") plt.show() if __name__ == "__main__": main() ```
code_blocks:
// A simplified pipelined hash lookup
module hash_lookup (
    input  wire        clk,
    input  wire [31:0] query,
    output reg  [31:0] result
);
    reg [31:0] mem [0:4194303];
    reg [31:0] addr_q;

    always @(posedge clk) begin
        addr_q <= (query ^ (query >> 16)) & 22'h3FFFFF;
        result <= mem[addr_q];
    end
endmodule
vlSelf->addr_q = (vlSelf->query ^ (vlSelf->query >> 16)) & 0xFFFFFFU;
vlSelf->result = vlSelf->mem[vlSelf->addr_q];
// <-- nothing here; next cycle's mem[] access will cold-miss
vlSelf->addr_q = (vlSelf->query ^ (vlSelf->query >> 16)) & 0xFFFFFFU;
vlSelf->result = vlSelf->mem[vlSelf->addr_q];
// auto-inserted when array exceeds size threshold and addr is a FF:
VL_PREFETCH_RD(&vlSelf->mem[vlSelf->addr_q]);
#!/usr/bin/env python3
"""
Benchmark: cross-cycle prefetch for FF-driven array addresses.

Typical IC pattern: pipelined hash lookup.
  Stage 1: addr_q <= hash(query)     [address pipeline register / FF]
  Stage 2: result <= mem[addr_q]     [memory read with registered address]

After eval() for cycle N, addr_q has been committed to hash(query_N).
That is exactly the address that mem[] will use next cycle.
"""

import os
import subprocess
import sys

try:
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    import matplotlib.ticker as ticker
except ImportError:
    subprocess.run([sys.executable, "-m", "pip", "install", "matplotlib"], check=True)
    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    import matplotlib.ticker as ticker

VERILATOR  = "../bin/verilator"
WORKDIR    = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(__file__))
NUM_CYCLES = 2_000_000

ARRAY_SIZES = [16384, 65536, 262144, 1048576,
               4194304, 16777216, 67108864, 134217728]

CACHE_L1   = 32   * 1024
CACHE_L2   = 512  * 1024
CACHE_L3   = 12   * 1024 * 1024
ELEM_BYTES = 4

# ── Verilog generator ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def make_verilog(tag, depth, mask):
    return (
        "// Pipelined hash-lookup - FF-driven address pattern.\n"
        "// addr_q is a pipeline register updated each cycle.\n"
        "// After eval(), addr_q == next cycle's mem[] index.\n"
        f"module hash_lookup_{tag} #(\n"
        f"    parameter DEPTH = {depth},\n"
        f"    parameter MASK  = {mask}\n"
        ") (\n"
        "    input  wire        clk,\n"
        "    input  wire [31:0] query,\n"
        "    output reg  [31:0] result\n"
        ");\n"
        "    reg [31:0] mem    [0:DEPTH-1];\n"
        "    reg [31:0] addr_q;\n"
        "\n"
        "    always @(posedge clk) begin\n"
        "        addr_q <= (query ^ (query >> 16)) & MASK;\n"
        "        result <= mem[addr_q & MASK];\n"
        "    end\n"
        "endmodule\n"
    )

# ── Testbench generator ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def make_tb(tag, mask, cycles):
    cls = f"Vhash_lookup_{tag}"
    dot = f"hash_lookup_{tag}__DOT__"
    return (
        "// Modes: rnd (no prefetch) | cross (cross-cycle prefetch)\n"
        "// cross: after eval(), prefetch mem[addr_q].\n"
        "// Simulates VL_PREFETCH_RD auto-inserted at end of _nba_sequent.\n"
        "#include <verilated.h>\n"
        f"#include \"{cls}.h\"\n"
        f"#include \"{cls}___024root.h\"\n"
        "#include <cstdint>\n"
        "#include <cstdio>\n"
        "#include <ctime>\n"
        "#include <cstring>\n"
        "\n"
        "static uint32_t lcg(uint32_t x) { return x * 1664525u + 1013904223u; }\n"
        "\n"
        "int main(int argc, char** argv) {\n"
        "    const bool cross = (argc > 1 && strcmp(argv[1], \"cross\") == 0);\n"
        "    VerilatedContext* ctx = new VerilatedContext;\n"
        f"    {cls}* top = new {cls}{{ctx}};\n"
        "    top->clk = 0; top->eval();\n"
        "    uint32_t sq = 1;\n"
        "    volatile uint32_t sink = 0;\n"
        "    struct timespec t0, t1;\n"
        "    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t0);\n"
        f"    for (int c = 0; c < {cycles}; c++) {{\n"
        "        sq = lcg(sq);\n"
        "        top->query = sq;\n"
        "        top->clk = 0; top->eval();\n"
        "        top->clk = 1; top->eval();\n"
        "        sink ^= top->result;\n"
        "        if (cross) {\n"
        "            // Cross-cycle prefetch:\n"
        "            // addr_q now holds hash(query_this_cycle) = next cycle's index.\n"
        "            // Equivalent to: VL_PREFETCH_RD(&vlSelf->mem[vlSelf->addr_q]);\n"
        "            __builtin_prefetch(\n"
        f"                &top->rootp->{dot}mem[\n"
        f"                    top->rootp->{dot}addr_q & {mask}u], 0, 0);\n"
        "        }\n"
        "    }\n"
        "    clock_gettime(CLOCK_MONOTONIC, &t1);\n"
        "    double ms = (t1.tv_sec - t0.tv_sec)*1e3 + (t1.tv_nsec - t0.tv_nsec)*1e-6;\n"
        "    printf(\"%.3f\\n\", ms);\n"
        "    (void)sink;\n"
        "    top->final(); delete top; delete ctx;\n"
        "    return 0;\n"
        "}\n"
    )

# ── helpers ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def run(cmd, **kw):
    r = subprocess.run(cmd, capture_output=True, text=True, **kw)
    if r.returncode != 0:
        print("STDERR:", r.stderr[-2000:])
        raise RuntimeError(f"Command failed: {' '.join(cmd)}")
    return r

def bench(depth):
    mask  = depth - 1
    tag   = str(depth)
    odir  = os.path.join(WORKDIR, f"obj_hash_{tag}")
    vf    = os.path.join(WORKDIR, f"hash_lookup_{tag}.v")
    tbf   = os.path.join(WORKDIR, f"tb_hash_{tag}.cpp")
    exe   = os.path.join(odir, f"sim_hash_{tag}")

    if not os.path.exists(exe):
        with open(vf,  "w") as f: f.write(make_verilog(tag, depth, mask))
        with open(tbf, "w") as f: f.write(make_tb(tag, mask, NUM_CYCLES))
        run([VERILATOR, "--cc", "--exe", "--build",
             "-o", f"sim_hash_{tag}", "--Mdir", odir, vf, tbf], cwd=WORKDIR)

    ms_rnd   = float(run([exe, "rnd"],   cwd=WORKDIR).stdout.strip())
    ms_cross = float(run([exe, "cross"], cwd=WORKDIR).stdout.strip())
    return ms_rnd, ms_cross

# ── main ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

def main():
    rnd_results   = {}
    cross_results = {}

    for depth in ARRAY_SIZES:
        mem_kb = depth * ELEM_BYTES / 1024
        label  = f"{mem_kb/1024:.0f}MB" if mem_kb >= 1024 else f"{mem_kb:.0f}KB"
        print(f"  depth={depth:>10} ({label:>6}) ... ", end="", flush=True)
        try:
            ms_rnd, ms_cross = bench(depth)
            rnd_results[depth]   = ms_rnd
            cross_results[depth] = ms_cross
            speedup = ms_rnd / ms_cross
            print(f"rnd={ms_rnd:7.0f}ms  cross={ms_cross:7.0f}ms  speedup={speedup:.2f}x")
        except Exception as e:
            print(f"FAILED: {e}")

    if not rnd_results:
        print("No results to plot.")
        return

    # ── plot ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
    fig, (ax1, ax2) = plt.subplots(1, 2, figsize=(15, 6))
    fig.suptitle(
        "Verilator: Cross-cycle Prefetch for FF-driven Array Access\n"
        f"Pattern: pipelined hash lookup  ({NUM_CYCLES:,} cycles, 1 lookup/cycle)\n"
        "addr_q (FF) updated each cycle => address known at end of eval()",
        fontsize=11, fontweight="bold"
    )

    xs = sorted(rnd_results)

    ax1.plot(xs, [rnd_results[x]   for x in xs], "o-", color="#F44336",
             linewidth=2, markersize=7, markerfacecolor="white",
             markeredgewidth=2, label="No prefetch (current Verilator)")
    ax1.plot(xs, [cross_results[x] for x in xs], "^-", color="#9C27B0",
             linewidth=2, markersize=7, markerfacecolor="white",
             markeredgewidth=2,
             label="Cross-cycle prefetch\n(proposed auto-insert at end of _nba_sequent)")
    ax1.set_xscale("log")
    ax1.set_xlabel("Table depth (32-bit entries)", fontsize=10)
    ax1.set_ylabel("Runtime (ms)", fontsize=10)
    ax1.set_title("Runtime", fontsize=10)
    ax1.legend(fontsize=9)
    ax1.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    ax1.xaxis.set_major_formatter(ticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{int(x):,}"))

    speedups = [rnd_results[x] / cross_results[x] for x in xs]
    ax2.plot(xs, speedups, "s-", color="#2196F3", linewidth=2, markersize=7,
             markerfacecolor="white", markeredgewidth=2)
    ax2.axhline(1.0, color="gray", linestyle="--", alpha=0.5)
    ax2.set_xscale("log")
    ax2.set_xlabel("Table depth (32-bit entries)", fontsize=10)
    ax2.set_ylabel("Speedup (x)", fontsize=10)
    ax2.set_title("Speedup from cross-cycle prefetch", fontsize=10)
    ax2.grid(True, alpha=0.3)
    ax2.xaxis.set_major_formatter(ticker.FuncFormatter(lambda x, _: f"{int(x):,}"))

    for ax in (ax1, ax2):
        for cache_bytes, lbl, color in [
            (CACHE_L1, "L1", "#4CAF50"),
            (CACHE_L2, "L2", "#FF9800"),
            (CACHE_L3, "L3", "#E91E63"),
        ]:
            boundary = cache_bytes / ELEM_BYTES
            if min(xs) <= boundary <= max(xs):
                ax.axvline(boundary, color=color, linestyle="--", alpha=0.5, linewidth=1.5)
                ylim = ax.get_ylim()
                ax.text(boundary * 1.05, ylim[1] * 0.97,
                        lbl, color=color, fontsize=9, va="top", fontweight="bold")

    plt.tight_layout()
    out_png = os.path.join(WORKDIR, "bench_hash.png")
    plt.savefig(out_png, dpi=150)
    print(f"\nChart saved -> {out_png}")
    plt.show()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    main()
